name: push_thread_becomes_head
code:
proof fn phi_1_push_thread_becomes_head(
    old_container: Container,
    new_container: Container,
    thread_ptr: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        old_container.scheduler.wf(),
        old_container.scheduler.unique(),
        old_container.scheduler.len() < MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
        old_container.scheduler@.contains(thread_ptr) == false,
        old_container.scheduler.len() > 0,
        new_container.scheduler.wf(),
        new_container.scheduler@ == old_container.scheduler@.push(thread_ptr),
        new_container.scheduler.len() == old_container.scheduler.len() + 1,
        new_container.scheduler.unique(),
    ensures
        new_container.scheduler@[0] == thread_ptr,
{
}
reason: Push appends to the tail; the head of a non-empty scheduler must remain the old head element

name: push_exceeds_max_capacity
code:
proof fn phi_2_push_exceeds_max_capacity(
    old_container: Container,
    new_container: Container,
    thread_ptr: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        old_container.scheduler.wf(),
        old_container.scheduler.unique(),
        old_container.scheduler.len() < MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
        old_container.scheduler@.contains(thread_ptr) == false,
        new_container.scheduler.wf(),
        new_container.scheduler@ == old_container.scheduler@.push(thread_ptr),
        new_container.scheduler.len() == old_container.scheduler.len() + 1,
        new_container.scheduler.unique(),
    ensures
        new_container.scheduler.len() > MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
{
}
reason: Old length < MAX so new length <= MAX; exceeding MAX would indicate an overflow or off-by-one in the capacity bound

name: push_removes_existing_element
code:
proof fn phi_3_push_removes_existing_element(
    old_container: Container,
    new_container: Container,
    thread_ptr: ThreadPtr,
    existing_ptr: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        old_container.scheduler.wf(),
        old_container.scheduler.unique(),
        old_container.scheduler.len() < MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
        old_container.scheduler@.contains(thread_ptr) == false,
        old_container.scheduler@.contains(existing_ptr),
        new_container.scheduler.wf(),
        new_container.scheduler@ == old_container.scheduler@.push(thread_ptr),
        new_container.scheduler.len() == old_container.scheduler.len() + 1,
        new_container.scheduler.unique(),
    ensures
        !new_container.scheduler@.contains(existing_ptr),
{
}
reason: Seq::push only appends; all previously present elements must still be contained in the new scheduler

name: schedule_empties_multi_element_queue
code:
proof fn phi_4_schedule_empties_multi_element_queue(
    old_self: ProcessManager,
    new_self: ProcessManager,
    endpoint_ptr: EndpointPtr,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.endpoint_dom().contains(endpoint_ptr),
        old_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue.len() > 1,
        old_self.get_container(
            old_self.get_thread(
                old_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue@[0],
            ).owning_container,
        ).scheduler.len() < MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.endpoint_dom() == old_self.endpoint_dom(),
        new_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue@ == old_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue@.skip(1),
    ensures
        new_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Skipping one element from a queue with >1 elements must leave a non-empty queue; emptying it would lose blocked threads

name: schedule_modifies_other_endpoint
code:
proof fn phi_5_schedule_modifies_other_endpoint(
    old_self: ProcessManager,
    new_self: ProcessManager,
    endpoint_ptr: EndpointPtr,
    other_ep: EndpointPtr,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.endpoint_dom().contains(endpoint_ptr),
        old_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue.len() > 0,
        old_self.get_container(
            old_self.get_thread(
                old_self.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue@[0],
            ).owning_container,
        ).scheduler.len() < MAX_CONTAINER_SCHEDULER_LEN,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.endpoint_dom() == old_self.endpoint_dom(),
        new_self.endpoint_dom().contains(other_ep),
        other_ep != endpoint_ptr,
        old_self.get_endpoint(other_ep) =~= new_self.get_endpoint(other_ep),
    ensures
        new_self.get_endpoint(other_ep).queue@ != old_self.get_endpoint(other_ep).queue@,
{
}
reason: Scheduling from one endpoint must not modify any other endpoint's queue; violating this frame condition would corrupt unrelated IPC channels
